violations.claim: VIOLATION: isInstalled() is a file-presence / signature check; it does not verify
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 467
; VIOLATION: isInstalled() is a file-presence / signature check; it does not verify
; the hook file has execute permission. "Hook installed" semantically implies the
; hook will fire on commit — that claim is false if the file is not executable.

(declare-const hook_file_exists   Int)   ; 1 iff .git/hooks/pre-commit file exists
(declare-const hook_is_executable Int)   ; 1 iff file has execute permission (+x)
(declare-const is_installed_result Int)  ; return value of isInstalled()

; Code transition: isInstalled() checks file presence (and possibly a shebang signature)
;   — it does NOT call stat() for execute bits
(assert (= is_installed_result hook_file_exists))

; Code transition: "Hook installed" is emitted when is_installed_result = 1
; (ternary resolves to that string literal)

; Violation: file exists → "Hook installed" logged, but hook cannot execute
(assert (= hook_file_exists    1))
(assert (= is_installed_result 1))
(assert (= hook_is_executable  0))

(check-sat)

violations.claim: [NEW] Ordering constraint: runHook --status is also callable immediately after
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 467
; [NEW] Ordering constraint: runHook --status is also callable immediately after
; runHook (default) in a pipeline (e.g., a shell script: `neurallog hook && neurallog hook --status`).
; The install() call in the first invocation mutates on-disk state; the second invocation's
; isInstalled() query reflects that mutated state. But if the first install() failed silently
; (returned a message without writing the file), isInstalled() returns 0 and logs "Hook not installed"
; even though the prior call already claimed success via result.message.

(declare-const install_call_1_succeeded  Int)   ; 1 = first invocation wrote the hook file
(declare-const is_installed_after_call_1 Int)   ; isInstalled() result in second invocation
(declare-const log_says_installed        Int)   ; 1 = "Hook installed" is output at line 467

; Code transition: install() in first invocation may fail silently (no error throw)
(assert (= install_call_1_succeeded 0))

; Code transition: isInstalled() reads disk state; file was never written → returns 0
(assert (= is_installed_after_call_1 (ite (= install_call_1_succeeded 1) 1 0)))

; Code transition: ternary resolves to "Hook not installed" when isInstalled() = 0
(assert (= log_says_installed is_installed_after_call_1))

; Violation: two consecutive CLI invocations produce contradictory observable outputs
;   (first: result.message says "installed"; second: "Hook not installed")
(assert (= install_call_1_succeeded 0))
(assert (= log_says_installed       0))

(check-sat)

(assert (= hook_file_exists    1))
(assert (= is_installed_result 1))

(check-sat)

; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 467
; [NEW] Ordering constraint: runHook --status is also callable immediately after
; runHook (default) in a pipeline (e.g., a shell script: `neurallog hook && neurallog hook --status`).
; The install() call in the first invocation mutates on-disk state; the second invocation's
; isInstalled() query reflects that mutated state. But if the first install() failed silently
; (returned a message without writing the file), isInstalled() returns 0 and logs "Hook not installed"
; even though the prior call already claimed success via result.message.

(declare-const install_call_1_succeeded  Int)   ; 1 = first invocation wrote the hook file
(declare-const is_installed_after_call_1 Int)   ; isInstalled() result in second invocation
(declare-const log_says_installed        Int)   ; 1 = "Hook installed" is output at line 467

; Code transition: install() in first invocation may fail silently (no error throw)
(assert (= install_call_1_succeeded 0))

; Code transition: isInstalled() reads disk state; file was never written → returns 0
(assert (= is_installed_after_call_1 (ite (= install_call_1_succeeded 1) 1 0)))

; Code transition: ternary resolves to "Hook not installed" when isInstalled() = 0
(assert (= log_says_installed is_installed_after_call_1))

; Violation: two consecutive CLI invocations produce contradictory observable outputs
;   (first: result.message says "installed"; second: "Hook not installed")
(assert (= install_call_1_succeeded 0))
(assert (= log_says_installed       0))
